channel.title: 2026-03-30 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-30 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Importance of Electronic Entropy for Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Martin Hoffmann Petersen, Steen Lysgaard, Arghya Bhowmik, Kedar Hippalgaonkar, Juan Maria Garcia Lastra
arXiv:2603.26471v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) enable large-scale atomistic simulations but remain challenged in describing mixed-valence materials where charge ordering strongly influences thermodynamic stability. Here we investigate the role of electronic entropy in MLIP structural optimization of the battery cathode material ȩNaFePO4. We show that conventional MLIPs fail to reproduce the correct stability of intermediate ȩNa concentrations because structural optimization leads to incorrect ȩFe²⁺/ȩFe³⁺ charge assignments, resulting in erroneous energy ordering and convex-hull predictions. Analysis of magnetic moments during structural optimization reveals that MLIPs are unable to capture electronic entropy associated with charge ordering.…

item.title: Reconstructing Quantum Dot Charge Stability Diagrams with Diffusion Models
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Vinicius Hernandes, Joseph Rogers, Rouven Koch, Thomas Spriggs, Brennan Undseth, Anasua Chatterjee, Lieven M. K. Vandersypen, Eliska Greplova
arXiv:2603.26432v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Efficiently characterizing quantum dot (QD) devices is a critical bottleneck when scaling quantum processors based on confined spins. Measuring high-resolution charge stability diagrams (or CSDs, data maps which crucially define the occupation of QDs) is time-consuming, particularly in emerging architectures where CSDs must be acquired with remote sensors that cannot probe the charge of the relevant dots directly. In this work, we present a generative approach to accelerate acquisition by reconstructing full CSDs from sparse measurements, using a conditional diffusion model. We evaluate our approach using two experimentally motivated masking strategies: uniform grid-based sampling, and line-cut sweeps. Our lightweight architecture, trained…

item.title: Design of expressive and trainable pulse-based quantum machine learning models
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Han-Xiao Tao, Xin Wang, and Re-Bing Wu
Author(s): Han-Xiao Tao, Xin Wang, and Re-Bing Wu Pulse-based quantum machine learning (QML) has emerged as a promising paradigm in quantum artificial intelligence due to its exceptional hardware efficiency. For real-world applications, it is crucial that pulse-based models are both expressive and trainable. Previous studies have shown that pulse-b… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 013338] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026

item.title: MarS-FM: Generative Modeling of Molecular Dynamics via Markov State Models
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kacper Kapuśniak, Cristian Gabellini, Michael Bronstein, Prudencio Tossou, Francesco Di Giovanni
arXiv:2509.24779v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Molecular Dynamics (MD) is a powerful computational microscope for probing protein functions. However, the need for fine-grained integration and the long timescales of biomolecular events make MD computationally expensive. To address this, several generative models have been proposed to generate surrogate trajectories at lower cost. Yet, these models typically learn a fixed-lag transition density, causing the training signal to be dominated by frequent but uninformative transitions. We introduce a new class of generative models, MSM Emulators, which instead learn to sample transitions across discrete states defined by an underlying Markov State Model (MSM). We instantiate this class with Markov Space Flow Matching (MarS-FM), whose sampli…

item.title: MDCrow: automating molecular dynamics workflows with large language models
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Quintina Campbell, Sam Cox, Jorge Medina, Brittany Watterson and Andrew D White
Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations are essential for understanding biomolecular systems but remain challenging to automate. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated success in automating complex scientific tasks using LLM-based agents. In this paper, we introduce MDCrow, an agentic LLM assistant capable of automating MD workflows for proteins. MDCrow uses chain-of-thought over 40 expert-designed tools for handling and processing files, setting up simulations, analyzing the simulation outputs, and retrieving relevant information from literature and databases. We assess MDCrow’s performance across 25 common tasks of varying complexity, and we evaluate the agent’s robustness to difficulty and prompt style. gpt-4o is able to complete increasingly complex tasks with l…

item.title: PFAS在女性卵巢储备功能减退中的毒性及作用机制解析：基于网络毒理学、机器学习、分子动力学模拟与体外实验验证的新见解
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Hengbing Li, Xin Hu, Zizhen Guo, Zhengao Sun
本研究综合网络毒理学、机器学习、分子对接和分子动力学模拟，探讨全氟和多氟烷基物质（PFAS）在女性卵巢储备功能减退中的潜在致病机制，并以KGN细胞进行后续验证。多数据库筛选得到35个候选靶点，富集分析显示PFAS可能扰动细胞增殖分化、免疫炎症反应及激素信号等关键通路。作者进一步识别出3个核心靶点，分子对接与动力学结果表明PFAS与这些靶点具有较强结合能力和稳定复合结构。

item.title: Physics-Informed Neural Network Approach for Surface Wave Propagation in Functionally Graded Magnetoelastic Layered Media
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Diksha, Katyayani, Hriticka Dhiman, Soniya Chaudhary, Pawan Kumar Sharma, Mayank Kumar Jha
arXiv:2603.25748v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper investigates propagation of SH-waves in a layered composite structure consisting of a pre-stressed functionally graded magnetoelastic orthotropic layer overlying a pre-stressed functionally graded orthotropic half-space under the influence of gravity. The study introduces a physics-informed neural network (PINN) framework for the dispersion analysis of SH-waves in the considered composite medium. As a benchmark, an analytical solution to the dispersion relation is derived and used to validate accuracy and reliability of the proposed PINN formulation. In the developed PINN model, the phase velocity corresponding to a prescribed wave number is treated as a trainable parameter, enabling the determination of the dispersion relation as…

item.title: 迈向 GUI 智能体：用于图形界面定位的视觉-语言扩散模型
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shrinidhi Kumbhar, Haofu Liao, Srikar Appalaraju, Kunwar Yashraj Singh
本文探讨离散视觉-语言扩散模型在 GUI 定位任务中的可行性，并将其作为传统自回归视觉-语言模型的替代方案。作者将 LLaDA-V 适配到单轮动作与边界框预测任务，并提出结合线性与确定性策略的混合掩码调度，以更好建模边界框几何的层次结构。实验在网页、桌面和移动界面等多个数据集上表明，该改进方法相较线性掩码版本具有更好的定位性能。

item.title: 用于经典规划的极值蒙特卡洛树搜索
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Masataro Asai, Stephen Wissow
本文研究经典规划中蒙特卡洛树搜索与多臂老虎机模型的适配问题。作者指出，面向有界奖励设计的 UCB1 不适合处理经典规划中取值无界的剩余代价估计，而高斯老虎机模型对其支持集刻画也不够精确。为同时解决这些问题，论文引入基于超阈值峰值极值理论的方法，提出新的老虎机算法 UCB1-Uniform，并给出遗憾界证明以及在经典规划任务上的实证结果。

item.title: 约束路径量子蒙特卡洛中超导配对-配对关联精度的基准评估
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jodie Roberts, Beau A. Thompson, R. Torsten Clay
该文针对 Hubbard 模型，评估了约束路径量子蒙特卡洛方法计算超导配对-配对关联时的精度问题。作者重点比较了常用的反向传播近似与新近提出的约束释放测量技术，并与可精确求解的体系结果进行对照。研究发现反向传播往往低估超导关联，而约束释放可提高准确性，但代价是计算开销显著增加并重新引入费米子符号问题。

item.title: SmoQyDQMC.jl：用于Hubbard模型与电子-声子相互作用的灵活行列式量子蒙特卡罗实现（2.0版）
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Benjamin Cohen-Stead, Shruti Agarwal, Sohan Malkaruge Costa, James Neuhaus, Andy Tanjaroon Ly, Yutan Zhang, Richard Scalettar, Kipton Barros, Steven Johnston
该文介绍了SmoQyDQMC.jl软件包2.0版本，这是一个基于Julia语言实现的行列式量子蒙特卡罗平台。程序支持广义紧束缚哈密顿量、局域与扩展Hubbard相互作用以及更一般的电子-声子耦合，包括非线性耦合与非简谐晶格势。新版采用带精确力的优化混合蒙特卡罗方法高效采样声子场，并提供灵活脚本接口，便于适配不同计算流程与Julia生态中的其他工具。

item.title: Interlayer coupling in two-dimensional MoS₂ and phosphorene bilayers: Benchmark quantum Monte Carlo study of interaction energies and quasiparticle band gaps
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Y. Huang, M. Manzoor, J. Brndiar, L. Mitas, P. R. C. Kent, and I. Štich
Author(s): Y. Huang, M. Manzoor, J. Brndiar, L. Mitas, P. R. C. Kent, and I. Štich The properties of 2D materials can be modulated by a host of different ways, one being a variable number of layers. Here, using ultra-accurate quantum Monte Carlo (QMC) methods, the authors study how the interlayer coupling modulates electronic properties in bilayers of MoS 2 and phosphorene. In bilayer MoS 2 , they find that the interlayer coupling is purely van der Waals and significantly weaker than in the bilayer phosphorene. Compared to the monolayer, QMC calculations indicate only a modest reduction of the band gap by 0.24±0.07 eV in the bilayer and a significant bias by both DFT and GW methods. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 115159] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026

item.title: [ASAP] Nuclear Spin–Spin Coupling Constants from Auxiliary Density Functional Theory
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Bernardo Zuniga-Gutierrez, Luis G. Cota, Jesús N. Pedroza-Montero, Mark R. Pederson, and Andreas M. Köster
Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation DOI: 10.1021/acs.jctc.5c02071

item.title: From ultrathin to bulk: decoding thickness-unrestricted ferroelectricity in Y:HfO 2 via first-principles
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Jin Huang, Jiangheng Yang, Shijie Jia, Junhui Wang, Fei Yan, Zhipeng Wang 等10位作者
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02046-5 From ultrathin to bulk: decoding thickness-unrestricted ferroelectricity in Y:HfO 2 via first-principles

item.title: Effect of Grain Size and Local Chemical Order on Creep Resistance in MoNbTaW Refractory High-Entropy Alloy: A Molecular Dynamics Study
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Saifuddin Zafar, Mashaekh Tausif Ehsan, Sourav Das Suvro, Mahmudul Islam, Mohammad Nasim Hasan
arXiv:2412.00588v5 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Refractory high-entropy alloy (RHEA) is a promising class of materials with potential applications in extreme environments, where the dominant failure mode is thermal creep. The design of these alloys, therefore, requires an understanding of how their microstructure and local chemical distribution affect creep behavior. In this study, we performed high-fidelity atomistic simulations using machine-learning interatomic potentials to explore the creep deformation of MoNbTaW RHEA under a wide range of stress and temperature conditions. We parametrized grain size and local chemical order (LCO) to investigate the effects of these two important design variables, which can be controlled during the alloy fabrication process, on creep deformation…

item.title: Vibrational energy relaxation of excited free OH stretching mode at the surface of ice: An ab initio molecular dynamics simulation study
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
The vibrational energy relaxation dynamics of the excited free OH bond on the ice surface are investigated using ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) simulations. The present AIMD study reproduces experimental results obtained via pump–probe sum-frequency generation spectroscopy. Simulations were conducted at 100 and 200 K for ice surfaces and compared with previous results at 300 K for water surfaces. The relaxation mechanisms were analyzed by selectively constraining specific vibrational modes, revealing distinct pathways: intramolecular stretch coupling, bend coupling, and intermolecular stretch coupling via reorientation or dipole–dipole interactions. At higher temperatures, intramolecular stretch coupling dominates, while at lower temperatures, the reorientation of the free OH is rest…

item.title: Microkinetic modeling of acidic corrosion from first principles and machine-learning molecular dynamics
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Ergen Bao, Wenjing Xu, Hui Ma, Yueqi Si, Ijaz Shahid, Yutong Huo 等9位作者
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02047-4 Microkinetic modeling of acidic corrosion from first principles and machine-learning molecular dynamics

item.title: 面向表面的体相通用原子间势微调：精度、效率与遗忘控制
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作聚焦于将原本面向体相材料的通用原子间势微调用于表面体系，以兼顾计算精度与效率。题目表明作者特别关注微调过程中对既有体相能力的“遗忘控制”，即在提升表面描述能力的同时尽量保留原模型的通用性。由于提供的摘要仅含出版信息，具体方法与结果仍需查阅原文。
